classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

source.title: NPNF1-11. Saint Chrysostom: Homilies on the Acts of the Apostles and the Epistle to the Romans

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “Brethren, my heart’s desire and prayer to God for them is, that they might be saved.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: He is now going again to rebuke them more vehemently than before.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Wherefore he again does away with every suspicion of hatred, and makes a great effort beforehand to correct misapprehension.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Do not then, he says, mind words or accusations, but observe that it is not in any hostile spirit that I say this.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For it is not likely that the same person should desire their salvation, and not desire it only, but even pray for it, and yet should also hate them, and feel aversion to them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For here he calls his exceeding desire, and the prayer which he makes (εὐδοκίαν), “heart’s desire.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For it is not the being freed from punishment only, but that they may also be saved, that he makes so great a point of, and prays for.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Nor is it from this only, but also from the sequel that he shows the goodwill that he hath towards them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For from what is open to him, as far as he can, he forces his way, and is contentious to find out some shadow at least of an excuse for them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And he hath not the power, being overcome by the nature of the facts.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “For I bear them record,” says he, “that they have a zeal of God, but not according to knowledge.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Ought not this then to be a ground for pardoning and not for accusing them?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For if it is not of man that they are separated, but through zeal, they deserved to be pitied rather than punished.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But observe how adroitly he favors them in the word, and yet shows their unseasonable obstinacy.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “For they being ignorant,” he says, “of God’s righteousness.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Again the word would lead to pardon.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But the sequel to stronger accusation, and such as does away with defence of any kind.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “And going about,” he says, “to establish their own righteousness, have not submitted themselves unto the righteousness of God.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And these things he says to show, that it was from a petulancy and love of power that they erred, rather than from ignorance, and that not even this righteousness from the deeds of the Law did they establish.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: 38; John. xii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For saying “going about to establish” is what one would do to show this.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And in plain words indeed he has not stated this (for he has not said, that they fell short of both righteousnesses), but he has given a hint of it in a very judicious manner, and with the wisdom so befitting him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For if they are still “going about” to establish that, it is very plain that they have not yet established it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: If they have not submitted themselves to this, they have fallen short of this also.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But he calls it their “own righteousness,” either because the Law was no longer of force, or because it was one of trouble and toil.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But this he calls God’s righteousness, that from faith, because it comes entirely from the grace from above, and because men are justified in this case, not by labors, but by the gift of God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But they that evermore resisted the Holy Ghost, and vexatiously tried to be justified by the Law, came not over to the faith.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But as they did not come over to the faith, nor receive the righteousness thereupon ensuing, and were not able to be justified by the Law either, they were thrown out of all resources.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “For Christ is the end of the Law for righteousness to every one that believeth.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: See the judgment of Paul.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For as he had spoken of a righteousness, and a righteousness, lest they of the Jews which believed should seem to have the one but be excluded from the other, and to be accused of lawlessness (for even these there was no less cause to fear about as being still newly come in), and lest Jews should again expect to achieve it, and should say, Though we have not at present fulfilled it, yet we certainly will fulfil it, see what ground he takes.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: He shows that there is but one righteousness, and that has its full issue in this, and that he that hath taken to himself this, the one by faith, hath fulfilled that also.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But he that rejects this, falls short as well of that also.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For if Christ be “the end of the Law,” he that hath not Christ, even if he seem to have that righteousness, hath it not.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But he that hath Christ, even though he have not fulfilled the Law aright, hath received the whole.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For the end of the physician’s art is health.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: As then he that can make whole, even though he hath not the physician’s art, hath everything; but he that knows not how to heal, though he seem to be a follower of the art, comes short of everything: so is it in the case of the Law and of faith.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: He that hath this hath the end of that likewise, but he that is without this is an alien from both.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For what was the object of the Law?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: To make man righteous.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But it had not the power, for no one fulfilled it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: This then was the end of the Law and to this it looked throughout, and for this all its parts were made, its feasts, and commandments, and sacrifices, and all besides, that man might be justified.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But this end Christ gave a fuller accomplishment of through faith.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Be not then afraid, he says, as if transgressing the Law in having come over to the faith.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For then dost thou transgress it, when for it thou dost not believe Christ.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: If thou believest in Him, then thou hast fulfilled it also, and much more than it commanded.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For thou hast received a much greater righteousness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Next, since this was an assertion, he again brings proof of it from the Scriptures.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “For Moses,” he says, “describeth the righteousness which is of the Law.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: What he means is this.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Moses showeth us the righteousness ensuing from the Law, what sort it is of, and whence.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: What sort is it then of, and what does it consist in?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: In fulfilling the commandments.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “He (R.T. the man), that doeth these things,” He says, “shall live by (or in), them.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: (Lev. xviii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And there is no other way of becoming righteous in the Law save by fulfilling the whole of it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But this has not been possible for any one, and therefore this righteousness has failed them. (διαπέπτωκεν).

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But tell us, Paul, of the other righteousness also, that which is of grace.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: What is that then, and of what does it consist?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Hear the words in which he gives a clear sketch of it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For after he had refuted the other, he next goes on to this, and says,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “But the righteousness which is of faith speaketh on this wise, Say not in thine heart, Who shall ascend into heaven (that is, to bring Christ down from above): or, Who shall descend into the deep? (that is, to bring up Christ again from the dead.)

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But what saith it?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: The word is nigh thee, even in thy mouth, and in thy heart, that is, the word of faith which we preach.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: That if thou shalt confess with thy mouth the Lord Jesus, and shalt believe in thine heart that God hath raised Him from the dead, thou shalt be saved.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: To prevent the Jews then from saying, How came they who had not found the lesser righteousness to find the greater? he gives a reason there was no answering, that this way was easier than that.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For that requires the fulfilment of all things (for when thou doest all, then thou shalt live); but the righteousness which is of faith doth not say this, but what?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “If thou confess with thy mouth the Lord Jesus, and believe in thy heart that God hath raised Him from the dead, thou shalt be saved.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Then again that we may not seem to be making it contemptible by showing it to be easy and cheap, observe how he expands his account of it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For he does not come immediately to the words just given, but what does he say?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “But the righteousness which is of faith saith on this wise; Say not in thine heart, Who shall go up into heaven? (that is, to bring Christ down); or, Who shall descend into the deep? (that is, to bring up Christ again from the dead.”)

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For as to the virtue manifested in works there is opposed a listlessness, which relaxeth our labors, and it requireth a very wakeful soul not to yield to it: thus, when one is required to believe, there are reasonings which confuse and make havoc of the minds of most men, and it wants a soul of some vigor to shake them thoroughly off.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And this is just why he brings the same before one.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And as he did in Abraham’s case, so he does here also.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For having there shown that he was justified by faith, lest he should seem to have gotten so great a crown by a mere chance, as if it were a thing of no account, to extol the nature of faith, he says, “Who against hope believed in hope, that he might become the father of many nations.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And being not weak in faith, he considered his own body now dead, and the deadness of Sarah’s womb.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: He staggered not at the promise of God through unbelief; but was strong in faith, giving glory to God; and being fully persuaded that what He had promised He was able also to perform” (Rom. iv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: 18–21): so he showed that there is need of vigor, and a lofty soul, that takes in things beyond expectation, and stumbles not at appearances.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: This then he does here also, and shows that it requires a wise mind, and a spirit heavenly (Gr. heaven-reaching) and great.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And he does not say merely, “Say not,” but, “Say not in thine heart,” that is, do not so much as think of doubting and saying with thyself, And how can this be?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: You see that this is a chief characteristic of faith, to leave all the consequences of this lower world, and so to seek for that which is above nature, and to cast out the feebleness of calculation, and so to accept everything from the Power of God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: The Jews, however, did not merely assert this, but that it was not possible to be justified by faith.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But himself turns even what had taken place to another account, that having shown the thing to be so great, that even after it had taken place it required faith, he might seem with good reason to bestow a crown on these: and he uses the words which are found in the Old Testament, being always at pains to keep quite clear of the charges of love of novelties, and of opposition to it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For this, which he here says of faith, Moses says to them of the commandment, so showing that they had enjoyed at God’s hand a great benefit.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For there is no need to say, he means, that one must go up to heaven, or cross a great sea, and then receive the commandments, but things so great and grand hath God made of easy access to us.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And what meaneth the phrase, “The Word is nigh thee?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: That is, It is easy.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For in thy mind and in thy tongue is thy salvation.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: There is no long journey to go, no seas to sail over, no mountains to pass, to get saved.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But if you be not minded to cross so much as the threshold, you may even while you sit at home be saved.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For “in thy mouth and in thy heart” is the source of salvation.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And then on another score also he makes the word of faith easy, and says, that “God raised Him from the dead.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For just reflect upon the worthiness of the Worker, and you will no longer see any difficulty in the thing.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: That He is Lord then, is plain from the resurrection.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And this he said at the beginning even of the Epistle.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “Which was declared to be the Son of God with power ... by the resurrection from the dead.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But that the resurrection is easy too, has been shown even to those who are very unbelieving, from the might of the Worker of it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Since then the righteousness is greater, and light and easy to receive, is it not a sign of the utmost contentiousness to leave what is light and easy, and set about impossibilities?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For they could not say that it was a thing they declined as burdensome.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: See then how he deprives them of all excuse.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For what do they deserve to have said in their defence, who choose what is burdensome and impracticable, and pass by what is light, and able to save them, and to give them those things which the Law could not give?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: All this can come only from a contentious spirit, which is in a state of rebellion against God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For the Law is galling (ἐπαχθὴς), but grace is easy.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: The Law, though they dispute never so much, does not save; Grace yieldeth the righteousness resulting from itself, and that from the Law likewise.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: What plea then is to rescue them, since they are disposed to be contentious against this, but cling to that to no purpose whatever?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Then, since he had made a strong assertion, he again confirms it from the Scripture.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “For the Scripture saith,” he proceeds, “Whosoever believeth on Him, shall not be ashamed.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For there is no difference between the Jew and the Greek; for the same Lord over all is rich unto all that call upon Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For whosoever shall call upon the Name of the Lord shall be saved.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: You see how he produces witnesses, whether to the faith, or to the confession of it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For the words, “Every one that believeth,” point out the faith.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But the words, “Whosoever shall call upon,” set forth confession.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Then again to proclaim the universality of the grace, and to lay their boasting low, what he had before demonstrated at length, he here briefly recalls to their memory, showing again that there is no difference between the Jew and the uncircumcised.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “For there is,” he says, “no difference between the Jew and the Greek.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And what he had said about the Father, when he was arguing this point, that he says here about the Son.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For as before he said in asserting this, “Is He the God of the Jews only?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Is He not of the Gentiles also?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Yes, of the Gentiles also: seeing it is one God” (Rom. iii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: 29, 30):—So he says here also, “For the same Lord over all is rich unto all (and upon all).”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: You see how he sets Him forth as exceedingly desiring our salvation, since He even reckons this to be riches to Himself; so that they are not even now to despair, or fancy that, provided they would repent, they were unpardonable.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For He who considereth it as riches to Himself to save us, will not cease to be rich.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Since even this is riches, the fact of the gift being shed forth unto all.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For since what distresseth him the most was, that they, who were in the enjoyment of a prerogative over the whole world, should now by the faith be degraded from these thrones, and be no wit better off than others, he brings the Prophets in constantly as foretelling, that they would have equal honor with them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “For whosoever,” he says, “believeth on Him shall not be ashamed” (Is. xxviii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: 16); and, “Whosoever shall call upon the Name of the Lord shall be saved.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And the “whosoever” is put in all cases, that they might not say aught in reply.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But there is nothing worse than vainglory.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For it was this, this most especially, which proved their ruin.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Whence Christ also said to them, “How can ye believe, which receive glory one of another, and seek not the glory which cometh of God only?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: This, with ruin, exposes men also to much ridicule and before the punishment in the other world involves them in ills unnumbered in this.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And if it seem good, that you may learn this clearly, leaving for the present the heavens which that puts us out of, and the hell which it thrusts us into, let us investigate the whole matter as here before us.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: What then can be more wasteful than this? what more disgraceful, or more offensive?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For that this disorder is a wasteful one is plain from the people who spend to no purpose whatsoever on theatres, horse-races, and other such irrelevant expenditures: from those that build the fine and expensive houses, and fit up everything in a useless style of extravagance, on which I must not enter in this discourse.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But that a person diseased in this way must needs be extravagant, and expensive, and rapacious, and covetous, anybody can see.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For that he may have food to give the brute, he thrusteth his hand into the substance of others.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And why do I talk of substance?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: It is not money only but souls also that this fire devoureth, and it worketh not death here only, but also hereafter.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For vanity is the mother of hell, and greatly kindleth that fire, and the venomous worm.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: One may see that it hath power even over the dead.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And what can be worse than this?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For the other passions are put an end to by death, but this even after death shows its force, and strives to display its nature even in the dead corpse.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For when men give orders on their death-bed to raise to them fine monuments, which will waste all their substance, and take pains to lay out beforehand a vast extravagance in their funeral, and in their lifetime insult the poor that come to them for a penny and a single loaf, but when they are dead give a rich banquet to the worm, why seek any more exorbitant thraldom to the disease?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: From this mischief also irregular loves are conceived.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For there are many whom it is not the beauty of the appearance, nor the desire of lying with her, but the wish to boast that “I have made conquest of such an one,” hath even drawn into adultery.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And why need I mention the other mischiefs that spring of this?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For I had rather be long (3 mss. διηνεκὥς) the slave of ten thousand savages, than of vanity once.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For even they do not put such commands upon their captives, as this vice lays upon its votaries.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Because it says, Be thou every one’s slave, be he nobler or be he lower than thyself.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Despise thy soul, neglect virtue, laugh at freedom, immolate thy salvation, and if thou doest any good thing, do it not to please God, but to display it to the many, that for these things thou mayest even lose thy crown.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And if thou give alms, or if thou fast, undergo the pains, but take care to lose the gain.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: What can be more cruel than these commands?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Hence grudging beareth sway, hence haughtiness, hence covetousness, the mother of evils.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For the swarm of domestics, and the black servants liveried in gold, and the hangers on, and the flatterers, and the silver-tinselled chariots, and the other absurdities greater than these, are not had for any pleasure’s sake or necessity, but for mere vanity.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Yes, one will say, but that this affliction is an evil, anybody can see; but how we are to keep quite clear of it, this is what you should tell us.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Well then, in the first place, if you persuade yourself that this disorder is a baneful one, you will have made a very good beginning towards correcting it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For when a man is sick, he speedily sends for the physician, if he be first made acquainted with the fact that he is sick.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But if thou seekest for another way besides to escape from hence, look to God continually, and be content with glory from Him; and if thou find the passion tickling thee, and stirring thee to tell thy well-doings to thy fellow-servants, bethink thyself next, that after telling them thou gainest nothing.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Quench the absurd desire, and say to thy soul, Lo, thou hast been so long big with thy own well-doings to tell them, and thou hast not had the courage to keep them to thyself, but hast blabbed them out to all.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: What good then hast thou gotten from this?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: None at all, but loss to the utmost, and avoidance of all that had been gathered together with much labor.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And besides this, consider another thing also, which is, that most men’s opinion is perverted, and not perverted only, but that it withers away so soon.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For supposing they do admire you for the time, when the occasion has gone by they will have forgotten it all, and have taken away from thee the crown God had given, and have been unable to secure to thee that from themselves.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And yet if this were abiding, it were a most miserable thing to exchange that for this.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But when even this hath gone, what defence shall we be able to make for betraying the abiding one for the sake of the unabiding one, for losing such blessings for the sake of credit with a few?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And indeed even if they who praise were numerous, even for this they were to be pitied, and the more so the more numerous those who do it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But if thou art surprised at what I have said, hear Christ giving His sentence in this way, “Woe unto you, when all men speak well of you.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And so indeed it should seem.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For if in every art you look to the workmen (δημιουργους) in it to be judges of it, how come you to trust the proving of virtue to the many, and not most of all to Him Who knoweth it more surely then any, and is best able to applaud and to crown it?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: This saying then, let us inscribe both on our walls and our doors and our mind, and let us keep constantly saying to ourselves, “Woe unto us, when all men speak well of us.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For even they that so speak slander one afterward as a vain person, and fond of honor, and covetous of their good word.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But God doeth not so.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But when He seeth thee coveting the glory that cometh of Him, then He will praise thee most, and respect (θαυμάσεται om. in most mss.) thee, and proclaim thee conqueror.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Not so man; but, when he finds thee slavish instead of free, by gratifying thee often by bare words with false praise, he snatches from thee thy true meed, and makes thee more of a menial than a purchased slave.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For those last men get to obey them after their orders, but thou even without orders makest thyself a slave.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For thou dost not even wait to hear something from them, but if thou merely knowest wherein thou mayest gratify them, even without their command thou doest all.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: What hell then should we not deserve, for giving the wicked pleasure, and courting their service before they give orders, while we will not hearken to God, even when He every day commands and exhorts us?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And yet if thou art covetous of glory and praise, avoid the praise that cometh of men, and then thou wilt attain to glory.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Turn aside from fair speeches, and then thou wilt obtain praises without number both from God and from men.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For there is no one we are used to give so much glory to, as the man who looks down upon glory, or to praise and respect so much as the man who thinks scorn of getting respected and praised.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And if we do so, much more will the God of the universe.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And when He glorifieth thee and praiseth thee, what man can be more justly pronounced blessed?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For there is not a greater difference between glory and disgrace, than between the glory from above and that of men.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Or rather, there is a much greater, aye an infinite difference.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For if this, even when it does not get put beside any other, is but a base and uncomely one, when we come to scrutinize it by the other’s side, just consider how great its baseness will be found to be!

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For as a prostitute stands at her place and lets herself out to any one, so are they that be slaves of vanity.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Or rather, these be more base than she.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For that sort of women do in many instances treat those enamoured of them with scorn.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But you prostitute yourself to everybody, whether runaway slaves, or thieves, or cut-purses (for it is of these and such as these that the play-houses that applaud you consist), and those whom as individuals you hold to be nothing worth, when in a body, you honor more than your own salvation and show yourself less worthy of honor than any of them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For how can you be else than less worthy, when you stand in need of the good word of others, and fancy that you have not enough by yourself, unless you receive the glory that cometh of others?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Do you not perceive, pray, beside what I have said, that as you are an object of notice, and known to every body, if you should commit a fault, you will have accusers unnumbered; but if unknown, you will remain in security?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Yes, a man may say, but then if I do well I shall have admirers unnumbered.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Now the fearful thing is, that it is not only when you sin, but even when you do aright, that the disorder of vanity does you mischief, in the former case subverting thousands, in the present bereaving thee entirely of thy reward.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: It is then a sad thing, and replete with disgrace of every kind, to be in love with glory even in civil matters.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But when even in spiritual you are in the same plight what excuse is there left remaining for you, when you are not minded to yield God even as much honor as you have yourself from your servants?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For even the slave “looketh to the eyes of his master” (Ps. cxxiii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: 2), and the hireling to his employer, who is to pay him wages, and the disciple to his master.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But you do just the contrary.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Having left the God that hired thee, even thy Master, thou lookest to thy fellow-servants; and this knowing that God remembers thy well-doings even after this life, but man only for the present.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And when thou hast spectators assembled in Heaven, thou art gathering together spectators upon earth.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And where the wrestler struggles, there he would be honored; but thou, while thy wrestling is above, art anxious to gain thee a crown below.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And what can be worse than madness like this?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But let us look, if it seem proper, at the crowns also.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For one is formed by haughtiness, and a second by grudging against another, and a third by dissimulation and flattery, another again by wealth, and another by servile obsequiousness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And like as children at their childish play put crowns of grass upon one another, and many a time laugh at him that is crowned behind his back; thus now also they that pass their praises upon thee, many a time joke by themselves at their putting the grass upon us.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And would it were grass only!

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But now the crown is laden with much mischief, and ruins all our well-doings.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Taking then the vileness of it into consideration, flee from the damage entailed.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For how many would you have to praise you?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: A hundred? or twice, or thrice, or four times as many?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Or rather, if you please, put them at ten times or twenty times as many, and let there be two or four thousand, or if you will, even ten thousand to applaud you.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Still these be no better than so many daws cawing from above.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Or rather taking the assemblage of the angels into consideration, these will seem more vile than even worms, and their good word of not so much solidity as a cobweb, or a smoke, or a dream.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Hear then how Paul, who saw through these things thoroughly, is so far from seeking after them, that he even deprecates them, in the words, “But God forbid that I should glory, save in the cross of Christ.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: This glory then be thou also emulous of, that thou mayest not provoke the Master, because in so doing thou art insulting God, and not thyself alone.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For if thou even wert a painter, and hadst some pupil, and he were to omit showing thee his practice of the art, but set forth his painting publicly just to any body that chanted to observe it, thou wouldest not take it quietly.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But if this even with thy fellow-servants were an insult, how much more with the Master!

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But if you have a mind to learn on other grounds to feel scorn for the thing, be of a lofty mind, laugh at appearances, increase thy love of real glory, be filled with a spiritual temper, say to thy soul as Paul did, “Knowest thou not that we shall judge angels?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: 3) and having by this roused it up, go on to rebuke it, and say, Thou that judgest the angels, wilt thou let thyself be judged of off-scourings, and be praised with dancers, and mimics, and gladiators, and horse-drivers?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For these men do follow after applause of this sort.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But do thou poise thy wing high above the din of these, and emulate that citizen of the wilderness, John, and learn how he was above regarding the multitude, and did not turn him to look at flatterers, but when he saw all the dwellers in Palestine poured forth about him, and wondering, and astonished at him, he was not puffed up with such honor as this, but rose up against them, and discoursing to his great concourse as if to one youth, he thus rebuked them and said, “Ye serpents, ye generation of vipers!”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Yet it was for him that they had run together, and left the cities, in order to see that holy personage, and still none of these things unnerved him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For he was far above glory, and free from all vanity.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: So also Stephen, when he saw the same people again, not honoring him, but mad upon him, and gnashing their teeth, being lifted above their wrath, said, “Ye stiff-necked and uncircumcised in heart.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Thus also Elias, when those armies were present, and the king, and all the people, said, “How long halt ye upon both your hips?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: (1 Kings xviii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: 21, LXX. true sense of “halt.”)

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But we flatter all, court all, with this servile obsequiousness buying their honor.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Wherefore all things are turned upside down, and for this favor the business of Christianity is betrayed, and everything neglected for the opinion of the generality.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Let us then banish this passion, and then we shall have a right notion of liberty, and of the haven, and the calm.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For the vain man is ever like persons in a storm, trembling, and fearing, and serving a thousand masters.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But he that is clear of this thraldom, is like men in havens, enjoying a liberty untainted.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Not so that person, but as many acquaintances as he has, so many masters has he, and he is forced to be a slave to all of them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: How then are we to get free from this hard bondage?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: It is by growing enamoured of another glory, which is really glory.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For as with those that are enamoured of persons, the sight of some handsomer one doth by its being seen take them off from the first: so with those that court the glory which cometh from us men, the glory from heaven, if it gleameth on them, has power to lead them off from this.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Let us then look to this, and become thoroughly acquainted with it, that by feeling admiration of its beauty, we may shun the hideousness of the other, and have the benefit of much pleasure by enjoying this continually.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Which may we all attain to by the grace and love toward man, etc.
